diagrams.title: Smart Meter Architecture Layers
diagrams.description: Shows three-layer architecture of intelligent energy metering system with bidirectional data flow
elements: Bottom Layer: Hardware (Smart Meters, Sensors, CTs/PTs)
Middle Layer: Communication (RF Mesh, PLC, Cellular, WAN)
Top Layer: Application (Analytics, Billing, Customer Portal, Demand Response)
Arrows showing two-way data flow between all layers
diagrams.purpose: Understanding system components and data flow in intelligent metering

diagrams.title: AMI System Architecture
diagrams.description: Comprehensive view of Advanced Metering Infrastructure showing utility to end-consumer connection
diagrams.purpose: Understanding complete AMI ecosystem and communication pathways

diagrams.title: Energy Data Flow Cycle
diagrams.description: Complete cycle showing how energy data is collected, processed, and used for optimization
diagrams.purpose: Understanding how raw meter data becomes actionable energy insights

diagrams.title: TOU Pricing vs Load Profile
diagrams.description: Graph showing typical daily load curve with TOU pricing zones overlaid
elements: Peak zone (6-9 AM, 6-9 PM) - Red with highest rates
Off-peak zone (9 PM - 7 AM) - Green with lowest rates
Shoulder zone (9 AM - 6 PM) - Yellow with moderate rates
Peak demand indicators at highest consumption points
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing how TOU pricing influences consumer behavior and reduces peak demand

diagrams.title: Smart Grid Integration with Energy Metering
diagrams.description: Shows how intelligent energy metering connects with smart grid infrastructure
elements: Data communication lines parallel to power lines
diagrams.purpose: Understanding the broader context of energy metering within smart grid ecosystems
